name: Between the Adour and the Gabas — loop from Saint-Sever
name_original: Entre l'Adour et le Gabas — boucle au départ de Saint-Sever
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Saint-Sever traverse a landscape characterized by rural scenery, undulating hills, and river valleys. The region features a mix of paved and unpaved paths, winding through woods, meadows, and agricultural areas. Cyclists can explore routes alongside waterways like the Adour and Gabas rivers, with some trails offering views of the wider Landes department's pine forests. The terrain provides options ranging from gentle rides to more challenging excursions with notable elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Sever The most popular touring cycling route is Between the Adour and the Gabas — loop from Saint-Sever, a 33.3 miles (53.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Saint-Sever Abbey – Saint-Sever Church loop from Saint-Sever, a difficult 24.6 miles (39.7 km) path that explores the area's cultural landmarks and surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Saint-Sever Church – Saint-Sever Abbey loop from Montaut, a 23.7 miles (38.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historic sites, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Sever is defined by rural landscapes, river valleys, and undulating terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Saint-Sever are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 350 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Sever's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Saint-Sever?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes around Saint-Sever, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists of all levels. These routes traverse rural landscapes, river valleys, and undulating terrain.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Saint-Sever?
A: Yes, Saint-Sever offers 22 easy touring cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35259580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Abbey – Saint-Sever Church loop from Villa Gallo-Romaine du Gleyzia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.8 km long with moderate elevation gain. The Chalosse Greenway, a 34-kilometer route following an old railway line, is also ideal for families due to its well-maintained, slightly undulating surface through pleasant rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Saint-Sever?
A: The touring cycling routes around Saint-Sever showcase a diverse landscape of rural scenery, undulating hills, and river valleys. You'll cycle through woods, meadows, and agricultural areas, often alongside waterways like the Adour and Gabas rivers. The wider Landes department also features extensive pine forests, offering shaded sections on some routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Saint-Sever area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Saint-Sever are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286899994/between-the-adour-and-the-gabas-loop-from-saint-sever" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between the Adour and the Gabas — loop from Saint-Sever&lt;/a&gt; is a 53.7 km circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10608150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Abbey – Saint-Sever Church loop from Saint-Sever&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What cultural landmarks or attractions can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Saint-Sever is rich in cultural heritage. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3553851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Abbey&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1018002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Church&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24831503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Church – Saint-Sever Abbey loop from Montaut&lt;/a&gt;, pass by these historic sites. The Chalosse Greenway also leads through charming villages like Montfort, Montaut, and Mugron.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5789369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chalosse Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas of the surrounding countryside. Many routes wind through hills and meadows, offering picturesque views of the rural landscape and river valleys.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Saint-Sever?
A: The routes around Saint-Sever cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 22 easy routes, 35 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes. Some trails feature significant elevation gain, while others, like the Chalosse Greenway, are relatively flat and easy to navigate.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Saint-Sever?
A: The touring cycling routes in Saint-Sever are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the mix of paved and unpaved paths, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Is there public transport access to the touring cycling trails in Saint-Sever?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to trailheads can vary, Saint-Sever itself is a central point in the region. For detailed information on public transport options to reach starting points, it's advisable to check local bus or train schedules for the Landes department.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Saint-Sever?
A: Saint-Sever, being a town, offers various parking options. You can typically find public parking near the town center or close to major landmarks like the Saint-Sever Abbey, which often serve as starting points for many cycling loops. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Saint-Sever?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring offers lush green landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and beautiful colors. Summer can be warm, but the presence of pine forests in the wider Landes region offers welcome shade on some routes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the Chalosse Greenway ('La voie verte') is a prominent example. This 34-kilometer route follows an old railway line, connecting Saint-Sever to the spa town of Dax. It offers a smooth, slightly undulating surface, perfect for a relaxed touring experience through rural landscapes.
tips: Thank you Dorothea for your tip! There was no service when we arrived, but the atmosphere touched us so much! So many architectural details to admire! Yes, and on the square in front of the cathedral we finally enjoyed café au lait et croissant again!
To see: the current castle and the ruins of the feudal castle.
This old railway line has been converted into a very cool greenway for the hot summer days. Please note that the greenway is not paved, it is not recommended to take it by road bike.
To leave Saint-Sever, this small road descends to take you to the heart of Chalosse.
